title: Centrosema triquetrum
intro: Centrosema triquetrum, also known as three-angled horsefly pea or buffalo pea, is a perennial legume native to Australia and Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Centrosema triquetrum include flavonoids, saponins, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Centrosema triquetrum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to alleviate symptoms of fever, nausea, and digestive discomfort.
taxonomy: Centrosema triquetrum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. It is a member of the subfamily Faboideae and the tribe Phaseoleae. The genus Centrosema is a group of tropical or subtropical legumes that are commonly found in warm and temperate regions. Within the Centrosema genus, C. triquetrum is one of the species that is widely distributed and adaptable. The classification of C. triquetrum reflects its unique characteristics and relationships with other plants in the Fabaceae family.
